name: motorBike (simpleFoam)
description: External aerodynamics around a motorcycle. Uses snappyHexMesh for body-fitted meshing and simpleFoam for steady-state RANS simulation. The classic OpenFOAM showcase case.
learning_objectives: Copy and prepare surface geometry (OBJ format)
Refine mesh around geometry with snappyHexMesh

mesh.title: Generate Mesh
mesh.description: Three-step meshing: surfaceFeatureExtract detects sharp edges, blockMesh creates the background hex mesh, then snappyHexMesh refines around the motorcycle geometry.
mesh.tip: snappyHexMesh reads motorBike.obj.gz from constant/triSurface/. The -overwrite flag replaces the background mesh in-place.

boundaries.title: Set Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Configure inlet velocity, outlet pressure, and wall conditions for the motorcycle surfaces.
boundaries.tip: The inlet uses fixedValue for U (typically 20 m/s). The motorcycle surfaces use noSlip for velocity.

solver.title: Configure Solver
solver.description: Set up simpleFoam with SIMPLE algorithm. This is a steady-state solver — endTime controls the maximum number of iterations.
solver.tip: For a quick test run, reduce endTime to 100 iterations. Full convergence typically needs 500+.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. Watch residuals — all fields should decrease below 1e-4 for convergence.
run.tip: This case takes longer than cavity. If residuals plateau, check boundary conditions and relaxation factors.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualize pressure distribution on the motorcycle surface, velocity field around it, and streamlines showing the wake.
postprocess.tip: In ParaView: apply 'Extract Block' to select motorcycle surfaces, then color by pressure. Use 'Stream Tracer' for flow visualization.
